things.name: Bryggen Wharf
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage row of Hanseatic timber buildings on the harbour front

things.name: Floibanen Funicular
things.description: Funicular to Mount Floyen at 320m with panoramic views and forest walks

things.name: Fish Market (Fisketorget)
things.description: Bustling outdoor market with fresh Norwegian seafood and local produce

things.name: Bergenhus Fortress
things.description: One of Norway's oldest and best-preserved medieval fortresses from the 13th century

things.name: KODE Art Museums
things.description: Four museums with outstanding art by Munch, Picasso, Monet and Norwegian masters

things.name: Hanseatic Museum
things.description: Authentic 14th-century Bryggen merchant house preserved as a living history museum

things.name: Ulriken Mountain
things.description: Bergen's highest mountain at 643m, accessible by cable car with spectacular views

things.name: Edvard Grieg Museum (Troldhaugen)
things.description: Home and studio of composer Edvard Grieg with regular summer concerts

things.name: Gamle Bergen Museum
things.description: Open-air museum of 55 wooden buildings recreating 18th and 19th-century Bergen

things.name: Fjord Cruises to Sognefjord
things.description: Day trips to the world's deepest fjord and the spectacular Naeroyfjord UNESCO site
